question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper describes the sequencing of HIV-1 PR/RT regions from patient samples. Specifically, it mentions that 89 out of 96 patients had their HIV-1 PR/RT regions sequenced.
sentences: "Among 96 patients recruited 89 (92.7%) had HIV-1 PR/RT regions sequenced"; "Among 96 ARV naïve patients recruited, 89 had both the PR and RT regions of HIV-1 sequenced (89/96, 92.7%)".

question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
explain: The paper does not mention any in vitro drug susceptibility testing. It focuses on the sequencing of HIV-1 PR/RT regions and the analysis of transmitted drug resistance mutations using the Stanford Surveillance Drug Resistance Mutation (SDRM) tool.
sentences: "Transmitted-drug-resistance was investigated by CPR tool (Stanford HIV-1 Drug Resistance/SDRM)"; "Susceptibility profile of ARV mutations was analyzed by the SDRM-2009 (Accessed: October/2013)".

question: What were the GenBank accession numbers for sequenced HIV isolates?
explain: The paper provides the GenBank accession numbers for the HIV-1 pol sequences described in the study, which range from KF700948 to KF701036.
sentences: "GenBank accession numbers of HIV-1 pol sequences described in this study are KF700948-KF701036".

question: Which HIV species were studied in the paper?
explain: The paper specifically mentions the study of HIV-1 transmitted drug resistance and genetic diversity among patients.
sentences: "HIV-1 transmitted-drug-resistance and genetic diversity are dynamic and may differ in distinct locations/risk groups"; "This cross-sectional study describes transmitted-drug-resistance and HIV-1 subtypes in protease/PR and reverse transcriptase/RT regions among antiretroviral naïve patients from Piauí State, Northeast Brazil".

question: Which HIV genes were reported to have been sequenced?
explain: The paper reports the sequencing of the protease (PR) and reverse transcriptase (RT) regions of HIV-1.
sentences: "Among 96 patients recruited 89 (92.7%) had HIV-1 PR/RT regions sequenced"; "The entire protease (PR) fragment and approximately 750-bp of the reverse transcriptase (RT) fragment were amplified".

question: From which countries were the sequenced samples obtained?
explain: The study was conducted in Piauí State, Northeast Brazil, and the samples were obtained from patients attending public health services in this region.
sentences: "This is a descriptive cross-sectional study designed to investigate the molecular epidemiology of HIV-1 among ARV naïve patients attending public health services in Piauí State, Northeast Brazil"; "In Piauí State, 3,958 AIDS cases were reported from 2009 to 2013 and during the study period (August 2011 to May 2012) 96 consecutive cases of HIV-1 ARV naïve patients were recruited".

question: From what years were the sequenced samples obtained?
explain: The study period during which the samples were obtained is specified as August 2011 to May 2012.
sentences: "In Piauí State, 3,958 AIDS cases were reported from 2009 to 2013 and during the study period (August 2011 to May 2012) 96 consecutive cases of HIV-1 ARV naïve patients were recruited".

question: What method was used for sequencing?
explain: The paper mentions that genomic sequencing was performed using the ABI Prism 3130 Genetic Analyzer, which is a platform used for Sanger sequencing.
sentences: "After purification of amplicons (QIAquick PCR Purification Kit/Qiagen, Hilden, Germany), genomic sequencing was performed (ABI Prism 3130 Genetic Analyzer, Applied Biosystems, Foster City, CA)".

question: Were samples cloned prior to sequencing?
explain: The paper does not mention any cloning of samples prior to sequencing. It describes the direct extraction of RNA, reverse transcription, and PCR amplification followed by sequencing.
sentences: "HIV-1 RNA was extracted from plasma (QIAamp Viral RNA Mini Kit, Qiagen, Hilden, Germany), reverse transcribed into complementary DNA (cDNA) (Invitrogen, Carlsbad, CA) and used as the target for a nested polymerase chain reaction (nested-PCR) of pol region as previously described".

question: Did samples undergo single genome sequencing?
explain: The paper does not mention the use of single genome sequencing. It describes the use of nested PCR and sequencing of the amplified products.
sentences: "HIV-1 RNA was extracted from plasma (QIAamp Viral RNA Mini Kit, Qiagen, Hilden, Germany), reverse transcribed into complementary DNA (cDNA) (Invitrogen, Carlsbad, CA) and used as the target for a nested polymerase chain reaction (nested-PCR) of pol region as previously described".
